market_context: SPY is +5.67% above its 20MA and +4.20% above its 50MA, sitting at 99.4% of its 52-week range (effectively at all-time highs) with a mild pre-market bid of +0.26%. QQQ is even stronger: +7.62% above its 20MA at 99.2% of range. IWM confirming breadth with small caps also at 52-week highs. Both index ETFs are in clean uptrends — bias is risk-on and bullish for today. The single outlier is NFLX, gapping -10.1% on a stock-specific catalyst; that trade runs counter to the broad market posture and is evaluated on its own merits.

trades.context: Netflix reported Q1 2026 after the close on April 16. EPS beat hard ($1.23 vs. $0.66 expected), but forward guidance disappointed: full-year operating margin guided at 31.5% vs. the 32.0% analyst consensus, and annual revenue kept at $50.7–51.7B vs. $51.38B expected — a 'beat-and-lower' pattern. Co-founder Reed Hastings simultaneously announced his departure from the board after 29 years, adding leadership uncertainty. Stock is gapping -10.1% in pre-market to ~$96.90, breaking decisively below the 20MA at $97.87. That MA now flips to resistance. Next technical target is the 50MA at $91.61.
if_conditions: NFLX opens at or below $97.87 (the 20MA) — gap must hold below this level at the open print
If NFLX bounces early, the bounce is rejected at or below $97.87 (20MA confirmed as resistance, not support)
SPY is not rallying more than +1.0% at 9:35am ET — a sharp market surge could drag NFLX into a gap-fill
No analyst upgrade or company statement reversing the guidance narrative appears before entry

trade.stop: -35% on premium OR NFLX trades and holds above $99.00 intraday

trades.invalidation: If NFLX opens above $98.50 and holds that level for 15 minutes, a gap-fill is underway and the bearish thesis is off the table — skip entirely.

trades.context: ASML reported Q1 2026 on April 15, beating on both revenue (€8.8B vs. €8.5B expected) and net profit (€2.8B vs. €2.5B expected) while raising its 2026 full-year guidance band to €36–40B from €34–39B. Despite the strong numbers, the stock sold off ~6% that day as investors fixated on tightening China export restrictions weighing on China revenue. Today ASML is recovering +2.42% to $1,444.98 in pre-market — suggesting the initial reaction was an overreaction to a headline risk that was already well-known. The stock remains solidly above both its 20MA ($1,379.99) and 50MA ($1,396.37), and the raised guidance is a real fundamental tailwind.
if_conditions: ASML holds above $1,410.83 (prior session close) at the open — the recovery must not reverse at the bell
Pre-market price of $1,435 or higher holds into 9:30am ET (no late pre-market fade)
No new China export control headline or policy announcement drops before or just after the open
SPY is not declining more than -0.8% at 9:35am ET

trade.stop: -35% on premium OR ASML price breaks back below $1,410.83

trades.invalidation: If ASML opens below $1,410 (prior session close), the post-earnings recovery has failed — do not enter. Note: each contract requires $4,500–$6,500 in capital; size accordingly.

trades.context: AMD closed at $278.26 yesterday — just $1.08 below its 52-week high of $279.34 — with volume running 1.82x the 20-day average, meeting the breakout screen threshold of >1.5x. The stock is 24.74% above its 20MA and 32.15% above its 50MA, confirming persistent upside momentum. In a risk-on tape with SPY and QQQ printing at 52-week highs, a clean break above $279.34 would set a new multi-year high for AMD. Pre-market is marginally softer (-0.35%), so entry requires confirming the $278 level holds on the open and that early volume supports the breakout.
if_conditions: AMD opens at or above $278.00 (prior close / 52-week high zone) and holds that level for 10 minutes without reversal
Volume in the first 15 minutes of trading is running above the average hourly pace (conviction required for a breakout)
SPY is not declining more than -0.8% at 9:35am ET

trade.stop: -35% on premium OR AMD price breaks below $275.00

trades.invalidation: If AMD opens below $276.00 or cannot hold $278.00 in the first 15 minutes, the breakout is failing — do not enter. The extension from the 20MA (+24.7%) also means mean-reversion risk is elevated if the tape turns.

skipped: TSLA — earnings April 22 (5 days out); pre-earnings IV will be elevated and the trend is mixed: above 20MA (+6.03%) but below 50MA (-0.39%), no clean directional setup
META — no specific catalyst today; HV30 of 46.7% makes options expensive without a defined event; above both MAs but at only 62.8% of 52-week range, not near a structural level
COIN — HV30 61.2% inflates option cost significantly without a specific catalyst; range position only 19.8% of 52-week band (no breakout setup); no event driver
PLTR — below both 20MA (-1.43%) and 50MA (-0.39%); no catalyst; bearish trend in a risk-on market does not present a clean directional trade
ORCL — +1.9% pre-market with elevated volume (1.7x) but HV30 of 61.5% makes calls very expensive relative to realized vol; no specific catalyst identified; range position only 26.1% of 52-week band
MSFT — +1.21% pre-market but no specific catalyst for today; generic momentum trade in absence of an event does not meet quality bar; earnings April 29 create upcoming IV inflation risk
